_meta.method: Hand-collected a small sample of U.S. lay sanghas from sangha homepages, regional directories, and Wake Up listings, focusing on those explicitly in the Plum Village / Thich Nhat Hanh tradition with clearly published in-person weekly meeting places.
_meta.coverageEstimate: ≈3–7% of active U.S. Plum Village / Wake Up sanghas with regular in-person meetings.
knownGaps: Most U.S. sanghas are only discoverable via the interactive Plumline map or local word-of-mouth; those data could not be crawled.
Many groups currently meet in hybrid or rotating home locations without a stable public street address, so they are excluded by design.
This sample does not attempt coverage of all regional Wake Up and all-ages sanghas in large metro areas (e.g., Bay Area, Chicago, Boston, Pacific Northwest), only a few illustrative cases.

places.name: Plum Blossom Sangha
places.address: Flow Yoga Westgate, 4477 South Lamar Blvd, Austin, TX 78745, USA
places.notes: Weekly hybrid practice in the Plum Village tradition every Sunday 4:00–6:00 p.m. at Flow Yoga Westgate, with sitting, walking meditation, readings from Thich Nhat Hanh, and Dharma sharing. Founded in the late 1990s as a Mindfulness Practice Community and continues to host retreats and Days of Mindfulness with Plum Village Dharma teachers.

places.name: Wake Up New York
places.address: Still Mind Zendo, 34 W 15th St, 3rd Floor, New York, NY 10011, USA
places.notes: Young-adult Wake Up sangha in Manhattan practicing in the Plum Village tradition; meets in person every Friday 7:30–9:30 p.m. at Still Mind Zendo with sitting and walking meditation, readings, and Dharma sharing. The group formed around 2011 after a Wake Up tour by Plum Village monastics and has since become a long-standing Wake Up hub for New York City.

places.name: Wake Up San Diego
places.address: WorldBeat Cultural Center, 2100 Park Blvd, San Diego, CA 92101, USA
places.notes: Wake Up sangha for young adults in the Plum Village tradition; holds in-person practice on Sundays at 4:00 p.m. at the WorldBeat Center in Balboa Park together with WorldBeat / HeartBeat Sangha, including meditation, walking, and sharing. The group grew out of earlier Heart Beat / World Beat PV practice in San Diego and has featured repeatedly in Wake Up's global communications as a model community.
